# Parabol — Full API Reference for LLMs

Parabol is a collaborative workspace that helps teams run more effective, inclusive, and engaging meetings.

## GraphQL API

**Endpoint:** `https://action.parabol.co/graphql`
**Schema SDL:** `https://action.parabol.co/graphql/schema.graphql`

## Authentication

Parabol uses Personal Access Tokens (PATs) for API access. Pass the token as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
```

Create and manage tokens at: https://action.parabol.co/me/profile

When creating a token via `createPersonalAccessToken`, you must specify:
- `name` — human-readable label
- `scopes` — list of `OAuthScopeEnum` values the token is permitted to use
- `expiresAt` — expiry date (max 1 year from now)
- `grantedOrgIds`, `grantedTeamIds`, `grantedPageIds` — optional restrictions (null = all accessible)

## Available Scopes

`users:read`, `users:write`, `teams:read`, `teams:write`, `orgs:read`, `orgs:write`,
`meetings:read`, `meetings:write`, `tasks:read`, `tasks:write`,
`templates:read`, `templates:write`, `pages:read`, `pages:write`,
`comments:read`, `comments:write`

Each mutation and query field is annotated with the required scope in the schema.

---

## Example Operations

### 1. Get the Authenticated User and Their Teams

```graphql
query GetViewerTeams {
  viewer {
    id
    name
    email
    teams {
      id
      name
      orgId
      isArchived
    }
  }
}
```

---

### 2. Start a Retrospective Meeting

Requires: `meetings:write`

```graphql
mutation StartRetro($teamId: ID!) {
  startRetrospective(teamId: $teamId) {
    meeting {
      id
      name
      meetingType
      phases {
        phaseType
        stages {
          id
          isComplete
        }
      }
    }
    error {
      message
    }
  }
}
```

Variables:
```json
{
  "teamId": "team123"
}
```

---

### 3. Create a Reflection During a Retrospective

Requires: `meetings:write`

Reflections can only be added during the **reflect phase** of an active retrospective meeting, before the group phase is complete. Each reflection answers a specific prompt (column) on the board.

```graphql
mutation CreateReflection($input: CreateReflectionInput!) {
  createReflection(input: $input) {
    reflectionGroup {
      id
      title
      reflections {
        id
        content
        promptId
      }
    }
    error {
      message
    }
  }
}
```

Variables:
```json
{
  "input": {
    "meetingId": "meeting123",
    "promptId": "prompt456",
    "content": "{\"type\":\"doc\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"We shipped the new onboarding flow on time.\"}]}]}",
    "sortOrder": 0
  }
}
```

> `content` is a stringified [TipTap](https://tiptap.dev/) JSONContent document (max 2000 characters). Omit for an empty reflection.
> `sortOrder` is a float used for positioning; use `0` to place at the top.

---

### 4. Add an Agenda Item to a Check-In Meeting

Requires: `teams:write`

Agenda items are discussion topics for the team's Check-In (action) meeting. Pinned items are automatically re-added to each subsequent meeting.

```graphql
mutation AddAgendaItem($newAgendaItem: CreateAgendaItemInput!) {
  addAgendaItem(newAgendaItem: $newAgendaItem) {
    agendaItem {
      id
      content
      pinned
      teamMemberId
    }
    error {
      message
    }
  }
}
```

Variables:
```json
{
  "newAgendaItem": {
    "teamId": "team123",
    "teamMemberId": "team123::user456",
    "content": "Review Q2 roadmap",
    "pinned": false
  }
}
```

> `teamMemberId` format: `<teamId>::<userId>`

---

### 5. Create a New Team

Requires: `teams:write`

```graphql
mutation AddTeam($newTeam: NewTeamInput!) {
  addTeam(newTeam: $newTeam) {
    team {
      id
      name
      orgId
    }
    error {
      message
    }
  }
}
```

Variables:
```json
{
  "newTeam": {
    "name": "Platform Team",
    "orgId": "org123",
    "isPublic": false
  }
}
```

---

### 6. Start a Sprint Poker Meeting

Requires: `meetings:write`

Sprint Poker is Parabol's estimation meeting type where the team collectively sizes backlog items.

```graphql
mutation StartSprintPoker($teamId: ID!) {
  startSprintPoker(teamId: $teamId) {
    meeting {
      id
      name
      meetingType
    }
    error {
      message
    }
  }
}
```

Variables:
```json
{
  "teamId": "team123"
}
```

---

### 7. Create a Task

Requires: `tasks:write`

```graphql
mutation CreateTask($newTask: CreateTaskInput!) {
  createTask(newTask: $newTask) {
    task {
      id
      content
      status
      teamId
    }
    error {
      message
    }
  }
}
```

Variables:
```json
{
  "newTask": {
    "teamId": "team123",
    "userId": "user456",
    "teamMemberId": "team123::user456",
    "status": "active",
    "content": "{\"type\":\"doc\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Update the API documentation\"}]}]}"
  }
}
```

> Task `content` is also a stringified TipTap JSONContent document.
> Valid `status` values: `active`, `stuck`, `done`, `future`

---

## Error Handling

Most mutations return a payload type with an optional `error` field:

```graphql
type StandardMutationError {
  title: String!
  message: String!
}
```

Always check `error` on the payload alongside your expected data fields. If the token lacks the required scope, the API returns a GraphQL error (not a payload error).

## Pagination

List fields on `viewer` (e.g. meetings, tasks) follow the Relay Connections spec with `first`, `after`, `edges`, `node`, and `pageInfo`.

## Rich Text Content

Reflection content, task content, and comment content are all stringified [TipTap](https://tiptap.dev/) JSONContent documents. The minimal valid document for plain text is:

```json
{
  "type": "doc",
  "content": [
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": [
        { "type": "text", "text": "Your text here" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Pass this object as a JSON-stringified string in the `content` field.
